items.title: Unsupervised learning paradigm
items.description: k-means is a canonical clustering algorithm under unsupervised learning: it discovers group structure from unlabeled X without predicting predefined target labels.

items.title: Distance metrics
items.description: Standard k-means minimizes squared Euclidean distance; neighbor assignments and centroid updates depend entirely on the metric geometry you impose on feature space.

items.title: Scaling and normalization
items.description: Euclidean k-means is scale-sensitive: preprocessing (standardization, normalization) defines which features dominate cluster formation and must be chosen before fitting.

items.title: Cluster evaluation
items.description: Choosing k and accepting a partition requires elbow plots, silhouette scores, stability checks, and external validation—workflows covered in cluster evaluation.
